title: Deliver a security-hardened papercuts fork
description:
## Outcome
Evolve the public 39elbarto/papercuts fork from upstream v0.1.0 into a security-hardened, agent-first CLI that can be adopted across many repositories without silently violating read-only boundaries, dirtying every worktree, or publishing sensitive context.

## Product constraints
Preserve the upstream append-only JSONL journal, deterministic machine contract, structured stdout/stderr, stable exit codes, concurrent writers, doctor diagnostics, and no-network logging. Prefer small upstream-friendly changes over a rewrite. No server, telemetry, automatic fixes, raw transcript storage, or ACFS-specific logic in the generic Rust core.

## Completion definition
All child decisions are recorded; safe single-project logging and review are implemented and adversarially verified; an allowlisted pilot produces evidence; multi-project review is implemented only after pilot approval; public release gates, attribution, compatibility, rollback, and secret scans pass. Each child bead must be independently executable from its own description and evidence requirements.
acceptance_criteria: Все обязательные дочерние phase gates закрыты доказательствами; публичный release воспроизводим и не содержит приватных данных; append-only и machine-contract инварианты сохранены; rollback проверен.
notes: Polished through seven passes on 2026-07-11: full plan conversion; dependency graph; structured acceptance and estimates; missing follow-up coverage; priority/self-containment repair; implementation ownership and test/evidence review; final br/bv steady-state audit. Exactly one child is ready: br-hardened-papercuts-fork-x30.1.

title: Inventory the upstream v0.1.0 contract and compatibility surface
description:
## Background
The fork starts from treygoff24/papercuts v0.1.0. Upstream treats docs/plans/2026-07-09-papercuts-design.md as normative and promises append-only JSONL, stable envelopes, contract version 1, deterministic IDs, bounded locks, explicit mutation annotations, and specific exit codes. Hardening must not silently invalidate those guarantees.

## Work
Trace CLI flags, environment discovery, record shapes, schema output, path capture, error mapping, read-only behavior, file creation, locking, fold semantics, and existing tests. Produce a compatibility matrix distinguishing unchanged upstream behavior, intended breaking changes, additive changes, and fork-only policy. Record which changes require contract-version movement. Inspect https://github.com/treygoff24/papercuts/issues/1 in full: committed-by-default sensitive-data/path leakage, undocumented effective Rust MSRV, the dangling absolute local skill symlink, and .git-marker repository detection. Classify each as generic upstream fix, fork hardening, documentation-only, or rejected with rationale.

## Evidence and tests
Use exact source/test references and representative command output. Run the unchanged upstream build, test, clippy, fmt, and doctor gate. Do not modify product behavior in this bead.

## Acceptance
A future implementer can understand every public compatibility boundary without rereading the original plan; unknowns are explicit; no implementation begins; evidence contains no secrets or unnecessary private paths.
acceptance_criteria: Матрица покрывает CLI, env/discovery, records, schema, errors, locking, side effects, tests и все четыре пункта upstream issue #1; baseline gate зелёный; продуктовый код не изменён.
notes:
Evidence: docs/UPSTREAM_V0_1_COMPATIBILITY_AUDIT.md
Baseline: release build PASS; 30 tests PASS; clippy -D warnings PASS; fmt PASS; papercuts doctor healthy with 2 lines.
Live upstream: main ffba2bd453ab0faeadf4f923fc727586958c8d6f; v0.1.0 5d8b827abbd054f5f506d26be865f5b7f573a298; issue #1 open without comments.
Scope proof: no diff under src/, tests/, Cargo.toml, or Cargo.lock.
Docs: docs/UPSTREAM_V0_1_COMPATIBILITY_AUDIT.md and docs/WORKLOG.md.
close_reason: Completed upstream v0.1.0 contract inventory and issue #1 classification. Product code unchanged; baseline gate passed. Docs: docs/UPSTREAM_V0_1_COMPATIBILITY_AUDIT.md, docs/WORKLOG.md.

title: Update schema, errors, and compatibility contract for hardening
description:
## Background
Agents self-orient through papercuts schema and stable JSON envelopes. New profiles, metadata, warnings, refusals, or record fields are unsafe if introduced without a precise machine contract.

## Work
Update serde output structs, schema inventory, command annotations, error/exit dictionary, examples, and compatibility/version behavior for the approved changes. Preserve stdout=data-only and stderr=errors-only. Pin representative instances and ensure help/version remain the only documented plaintext exceptions. Document legacy reading and mixed-journal behavior.

## Tests
Deserialization tests for every success/error shape; schema snapshot/semantic assertions; invalid-argument and suggested-fix cases; safe/legacy examples; unknown-field/old-record compatibility. Verify diagnostics never contain redacted source values or hidden paths.

## Acceptance
Schema is the single self-orientation source, cannot drift from error definitions, and truthfully labels every command as read-only/appending/destructive. Contract/version change is explicit, tested, and migration-documented.
design: Treat src/error.rs and schema generation as single sources of truth; derive published dictionaries where possible. Integrate only after profile/path/secret shapes stabilize. Do not duplicate constants across docs and code.
acceptance_criteria: Каждый shape/error/exit/profile присутствует в self-orienting schema и pinned tests; version transition явный; stdout/stderr contract и old-record compatibility сохранены.

title: Build adversarial unit and end-to-end security acceptance suite
description:
## Outcome
Create a comprehensive test gate that validates the hardened user workflow as a real binary, not only isolated functions.

## Coverage
Exercise clean first run, safe and legacy profiles, env/flag precedence, add from argv/stdin, refusal and override, path minimization, list/resolve/doctor/schema, malformed/torn/mixed old-new journals, permissions, missing HOME, git worktrees, concurrent distinct/identical adds, lock timeout, and no-write behavior. Use only synthetic data and disposable temp roots.

## Logging
Provide an e2e runner or clearly grouped integration tests with detailed but sanitized phase logs: preflight, command, exit, expected side effects, verification, and cleanup. On failure, retain a bounded artifact directory with commands and redacted output; never retain synthetic secret literal values beyond controlled fixtures.

## Acceptance
Every hardened feature maps to unit and e2e assertions; regression coverage preserves upstream behavior; tests prove rejected inputs do not persist; concurrency/storage tests run five times when touched; full cargo gate and scoped UBS pass with evidence recorded.
acceptance_criteria: Реальный binary проходит полный safe/legacy lifecycle, no-write/refusal, corruption, permission, worktree и concurrency cases; sanitized detailed logs и bounded failure artifacts доступны; regressions отсутствуют.

title: Publish safe single-project instructions and operator review runbook
description:
## Outcome
Give humans and agents an accurate, copy-pasteable adoption path for one repository after hardening is implemented.

## Work
Update README and provide canonical AGENTS.md snippets that distinguish writable logging from read-only/audit-only tasks; explain private/safe default, legacy opt-in, tags/severity, redaction limitations, doctor/list/resolve lifecycle, and how not to interrupt the main task. Add an operator runbook with prerequisites, preflight, execution, verification, rollback, evidence, and review cadence. Clearly label planned versus implemented behavior.

## Tests
Run every documented command against the built binary in a disposable repo. Add documentation contract tests or e2e assertions where practical. Secret/path examples must be synthetic and must not train agents to paste raw sensitive data.

## Acceptance
A new user can install/configure, log, review, resolve, and roll back without hidden local knowledge; read-only boundary is explicit; docs match schema and actual CLI output.
acceptance_criteria: Все documented commands проверены в disposable repo; AGENTS snippet уважает read-only boundaries; install/log/review/resolve/rollback выполнимы без скрытого знания; docs не обещают несуществующую защиту.

title: Run the hardened single-project release gate
description:
## Outcome
Decide whether the fork is genuinely safe enough for an allowlisted pilot. This is an evidence gate, not a ceremonial checklist.

## Preflight
Confirm approved ADR, compatibility strategy, implemented profiles/path/secret behavior, schema, tests, docs, and rollback. Fetch upstream and record divergence without modifying history unexpectedly.

## Verification
Run cargo build release, all-feature tests, clippy -D warnings, fmt, papercuts doctor, the adversarial e2e suite, five-run concurrency sweep when relevant, scoped UBS, dependency review, and gitleaks over the intended public diff/history. Manually inspect outputs for path/secret leakage. Capture versions, commit SHA, commands, and bounded results.

## Acceptance
Zero unexplained failures or leaks; every plan acceptance item is traced to evidence; compatibility and rollback are exercised; any waiver is explicit and blocks pilot unless risk is accepted in a recorded decision.
acceptance_criteria: Все обязательные команды и security inspections имеют commit-pinned evidence; нет unexplained failures/leaks; compatibility и rollback реально выполнены; без gate pilot заблокирован.

title: Design a reversible allowlisted pilot and installation lane
description:
## Background
Wider rollout must follow evidence from a small pilot. Candidate projects are papercuts, acfs-workbench, and at most two other active repositories. The forked binary must be unambiguous and reversible.

## Work
Choose installation/version naming, allowlisted repositories, private log locations/profiles, canonical AGENTS snippets, observation period, success/failure metrics, support/rollback steps, and evidence retention. Prevent PATH ambiguity with upstream v0.1.0. Define how read-only tasks report friction without mutation.

## Acceptance
The pilot plan names exact repos and owners, commands for install/preflight/verification/rollback, metrics for useful cuts/noise/false positives/leaks, stop conditions, and a bounded duration. No repository is modified by this planning bead.
acceptance_criteria: Allowlist максимум из четырёх проектов, versioned install, metrics, duration, stop conditions, rollback и evidence retention указаны; planning bead не меняет целевые repos.

title: Execute the allowlisted pilot and collect sanitized evidence
description:
## Outcome
Run the approved hardened binary in the allowlisted projects long enough to learn whether it captures useful friction without creating security, noise, or worktree problems.

## Work
Apply only approved instructions/configuration, verify each repo before and after, collect counts and categories rather than raw private content where possible, exercise review/resolve/rollback, and record false positives, misses, command friction, agent compliance, and operator time. Do not widen beyond the allowlist.

## Evidence
Maintain a sanitized ledger with binary SHA/version, repo key, profile, test dates, counts, failures, mitigations, and rollback results. Any suspected disclosure stops the pilot and triggers cleanup/incident handling outside public logs.

## Acceptance
All pilot scenarios complete or stop safely; no unexplained repository mutation or sensitive publication; evidence is sufficient for a go/no-go decision; every change has a verified rollback.
acceptance_criteria: Каждый allowlisted repo имеет before/after и rollback proof; собраны sanitized signal/noise/false-positive/operator-cost данные; scope не расширен; disclosure немедленно останавливает pilot.

title: Decide wider adoption from pilot evidence
description:
## Outcome
Make a documented go, revise-and-repeat, or stop decision using pilot evidence rather than enthusiasm.

## Analysis
Evaluate signal quality, duplicate/noise rate, false positives/known misses, agent compliance, read-only behavior, worktree impact, operator review cost, rollback reliability, and security observations. Decide whether to widen single-project use and whether multi-project review is justified.

## Acceptance
Decision cites sanitized evidence, defines residual risks and next scope, updates docs/worklog, and either authorizes the next phase or creates explicit remediation beads. No automatic global rollout occurs in this bead.
acceptance_criteria: На основе evidence принято go/revise/stop решение с residual risks; следующий scope или remediation beads явны; глобальный rollout не выполняется автоматически.

title: Design an explicit allowlisted multi-project inventory contract
description:
## Background
Cross-project review can identify repeated friction, but unrestricted filesystem scanning would violate project boundaries and collect private evidence. Source journals must remain authoritative.

## Work
Define an explicit allowlist/inventory format, stable project keys, journal locations, aliases, failure isolation, retention, permissions, output bounds, and JSON/Markdown digest contract. Specify discovery that never traverses arbitrary roots, clustering that never rewrites journals, and safe handling of missing/corrupt/locked logs. Decide whether this is a separate binary/subcommand/thin adapter.

## Acceptance
Threat model, examples, schema, compatibility, preflight, rollback, and unit/e2e cases are explicit. ACFS/ClickUp/CM details stay outside the generic core unless proven generic.
acceptance_criteria: Inventory строго allowlisted, не сканирует arbitrary roots, определяет project keys/aliases/bounds/errors/retention/schema/rollback и полный privacy-focused test plan.

title: Implement allowlisted multi-project digest with bounded output
description:
## Outcome
Produce deterministic JSON and Markdown review digests from only explicitly configured project journals while keeping each source journal authoritative and unchanged.

## Scope
Implement approved inventory parsing, alias/project-key handling, bounded concurrency/output, per-project error isolation, status/tag/severity filters, deterministic ordering, and sanitized diagnostics. No arbitrary recursive scanning, network sync, journal rewriting, or automatic resolution.

## Tests and evidence
Unit-test inventory validation and stable ordering; e2e multiple disposable repos including missing, corrupt, locked, private, and mixed-version logs; prove no unlisted path is opened; log detailed sanitized phases and cleanup. Document preflight, verification, rollback, and evidence.

## Acceptance
Outputs match schema; sources remain byte-identical; failures are isolated and machine-readable; privacy boundaries are tested; cargo gate and scoped UBS pass.
design: Prefer an isolated inventory/digest module or thin separate command surface. Reuse read/fold APIs without widening default repo discovery. Source journals are read-only inputs; inventory configuration is the only discovery authority.
acceptance_criteria: Unlisted paths не открываются; source journals byte-identical; deterministic bounded JSON/Markdown digest и per-project failure isolation покрыты e2e; cargo gate и UBS зелёные.

title: Define and pilot promotion rules for issues, runbooks, CM, and ClickUp
description:
## Background
A papercut journal is useful only if repeated friction can become durable improvement work. Destinations have different semantics: repository bugs/tasks, runbooks/helpers, cross-agent CM rules, and human project work in ClickUp. Automatic writeback would be dangerous.

## Work
Define evidence thresholds, deduplication, human approval, destination selection, reference recording on resolution, failure/rollback, and idempotency. Start with payload generation or thin adapters; no automatic bulk writes. Keep ACFS/Beads/ClickUp/CM integration outside the generic Rust core unless it is universally applicable.

## Tests
Use dry-run/payload-only fixtures first. For any live connector pilot, require explicit scope, one-record write, readback, cleanup/rollback, and no secrets. Verify a successful API response with independent readback.

## Acceptance
A concise decision/runbook exists; only adapters justified by repeated pilot evidence are implemented; unresolved or declined suggestions remain traceable without rewriting source journals. This bead does not block the first generic public release unless the release scope explicitly includes adapters.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence thresholds, approval, dedup, dry-run payloads, readback and rollback определены; live writes только one-record and verified; generic core не загрязнён ACFS-specific logic.

title: Decide safe storage profiles and read-only task semantics
description:
## Background
Upstream discovers a repository and writes .papercuts.jsonl there by default. That makes friction visible in diffs, but it can dirty repositories and conflicts with audit-only/read-only work. The fork needs safe defaults without pretending a CLI can infer every conversational authorization boundary.

## Work
Define named or flag-based storage profiles, precedence across flag/environment/defaults, first-run behavior, repository-local versus user-state locations, and an explicit legacy compatibility path. Decide what the CLI can enforce versus what AGENTS.md policy must enforce. Specify whether read-only commands may discover without creating directories and how mutation attempts should fail or warn under a restricted profile.

## Required analysis
Compare private-by-default, committed-by-default with warnings, and explicit-profile-only designs. Cover existing logs, non-git directories, worktrees/submodules, missing HOME, permission errors, and migration rollback. No network or persistent daemon.

## Acceptance
An ADR-quality decision selects one default and one migration story; precedence and failure behavior are unambiguous; schema implications and black-box test cases are enumerated; no fake guarantee is made that the CLI knows a task was declared read-only.
acceptance_criteria: Выбран один safe default и explicit legacy mode; precedence, no-write semantics, migration, rollback и black-box cases зафиксированы; CLI не приписывается способность понимать conversational read-only scope.
notes:
Baseline audit: docs/UPSTREAM_V0_1_COMPATIBILITY_AUDIT.md.
Facts to carry into this decision: v0.1 discovery is --file > PAPERCUTS_FILE > nearest .git.exists() > HOME; repo-local committed storage is intentional; list/doctor treat a missing discovered file as virtual empty and do not create it; add --dry-run creates nothing; actual add creates parent/file; doctor marks a gitignored journal unhealthy. CLI cannot infer conversational read-only scope. Changing the default storage behavior should be explicitly versioned with a legacy path.

title: Prepare and publish the first hardened public fork release
description:
## Outcome
Publish a transparent hardened fork release only after the approved functional phases and public safety gates pass.

## Work
Settle version/name/install method; synchronize or document upstream divergence; update compatibility matrix, README, migration and rollback instructions, changelog, attribution, and release notes. Do not publish to crates.io or another namespace without verified ownership and explicit decision. Clearly distinguish upstream behavior, implemented fork hardening, optional multi-project capability, and deferred adapters.

## Release gate
Run full Rust quality gate, adversarial/e2e suite, doctor, UBS, dependency review, license/attribution audit, and gitleaks over public history and artifacts. Verify clean checkout installation and smoke lifecycle. Record commit/tag/artifact hashes and rollback.

## Acceptance
Public GitHub release is reproducible, contains no private operational material, makes no unsupported security claim, links upstream, documents compatibility and known limitations, and has a tested uninstall/rollback path.
acceptance_criteria: Clean-checkout install и smoke lifecycle воспроизводимы; cargo/e2e/UBS/dependency/license/gitleaks gates зелёные; tag/artifact hashes записаны; attribution, limitations, migration and rollback опубликованы.

title: Define tag vocabulary and review lifecycle
description:
## Background
The pilot must turn raw cuts into a manageable review practice. Free-form tags fragment easily, duplicate cuts can hide recurring friction, and resolution without a durable reference makes it hard to prove improvement. The project plan calls for configurable tag vocabulary, review cadence, and promotion references.

## Work
Using sanitized pilot evidence, define a small canonical tag taxonomy with extension rules; severity guidance; duplicate/recurrence handling; review cadence; states for fixed, declined, superseded, or promoted outcomes; and how resolution notes reference a Bead, issue, runbook, commit, CM rule, or ClickUp workstream without embedding private URLs/data in public journals. Decide which behavior belongs in generic CLI schema versus operator documentation or adapters.

## Tests and evidence
Replay representative synthetic/pilot-derived categories through list/digest workflows. Verify unknown/custom tags remain forward-compatible, deterministic sorting survives, and no taxonomy migration rewrites append-only history.

## Acceptance
The lifecycle is small enough for daily use, distinguishes papercuts from bugs/incidents/completed work, supports local extensions, defines review and evidence rules, and provides migration/rollback without journal rewrites.
acceptance_criteria: Canonical tags, extension rules, severity, duplicate handling, review cadence and resolution references are evidence-backed, deterministic and append-only compatible; boundaries to bugs/incidents/worklogs are explicit.

title: Decide path minimization and repository identity contract
description:
## Background
Upstream cut records capture absolute cwd and repo paths. These can reveal usernames and internal layouts when journals are committed. Multi-project review also needs a stable identity without unrestricted filesystem discovery.

## Work
Choose among omission, repository-relative paths, stable project keys, salted hashes, or explicit modes. Specify defaults, compatibility, serialization shape, symlink/worktree behavior, paths outside the repo, non-UTF8 handling, and whether existing contract-1 readers tolerate the change. Define how a later allowlisted inventory identifies projects without embedding sensitive absolute paths in public records.

## Security constraints
Safe mode must minimize metadata before content scanning. Errors and warnings must not echo a path the selected policy intended to hide. Do not create a reversible fingerprint that merely disguises a private path.

## Acceptance
The decision includes exact example records for safe and legacy modes, collision/privacy analysis, schema/version consequences, migration and rollback, and focused unit plus black-box tests.
acceptance_criteria: Есть точные safe/legacy record examples, privacy and collision analysis, symlink/worktree rules, schema/version impact, migration, rollback и тестовая матрица.
notes:
Baseline audit: docs/UPSTREAM_V0_1_COMPATIBILITY_AUDIT.md.
Facts to carry into this decision: contract-1 CutRecord requires cwd:String and repo:Option<String>; add records lossy absolute cwd/repo; success meta.file and path-bearing errors also reveal resolved paths; paths are not part of the content ID; .git existence alone defines repo root. Omitting/replacing required path fields or changing default discovery is an observable contract change; path minimization must cover records, meta, warnings, and errors.

title: Define deterministic sensitive-data guardrail behavior
description:
## Background
Cut text is agent-authored free form. A token, credential URL, key fragment, email, customer identifier, or raw config could be appended and pushed. Detection is a guardrail, never proof of safety.

## Work
Define a bounded, local, deterministic preflight: threat classes, pattern sources, entropy use if any, allowlist/suppression rules, size limits, warn-versus-refuse policy, override semantics, dry-run behavior, redacted diagnostics, and structured error/schema contract. Decide how to avoid echoing the suspected secret and how to explain false positives without exposing values.

## Required adversarial cases
Bearer/basic credentials, common cloud/token prefixes, PEM material, credential-bearing URLs, emails/identifiers, benign hashes, test fixtures, shell snippets, Unicode, multiline input, very long input, and encoded material. Explicitly document misses that remain possible.

## Acceptance
A reviewed policy matrix and test corpus specification exist; default behavior is selected; override is deliberate and auditable; no raw suspect value appears in output/logs; performance and offline constraints are stated.
acceptance_criteria: Policy matrix определяет warn/refuse/override, redacted diagnostics, bounded offline checks, false-positive controls, synthetic corpus и честные limitations; подозрительное значение нигде не эхоится.
notes:
Baseline audit: docs/UPSTREAM_V0_1_COMPATIBILITY_AUDIT.md.
Facts to carry into this decision: add accepts any non-empty UTF-8 text up to 10,000 bytes and persists it unchanged; there is no credential/PII/path preflight; default storage is a tracked repo file; error/schema outputs are structured and deterministic. A default refusal of previously accepted text is a behavioral break and needs an explicit hardened-version plus legacy/override story. Suspect values must never be echoed.

title: Publish the hardened contract ADR and converge the plan
description:
## Outcome
Integrate the approved storage, path, and sensitive-data decisions into one self-contained hardened contract before implementation.

## Work
Update the project plan and add an ADR that defines user workflows, CLI/profile resolution, record metadata, schema/version policy, threat model, compatibility mode, rollback, and ownership boundaries between Rust mechanism and AGENTS.md policy. Reconcile contradictions with the upstream normative design explicitly instead of silently editing promises. Include concrete command and JSON examples plus rejected alternatives and rationale.

## Review protocol
Perform multiple fresh-context review passes until changes become incremental. Check usability, security honesty, deterministic behavior, append-only invariants, and migration. Separate generic upstream candidates from fork-only policy.

## Beads propagation gate
Before closing this bead, use br update to copy the approved default, exact flags/env precedence, record examples, error/version behavior, rollback, and forbidden actions into every affected implementation/test/docs bead. A downstream agent must not need PROJECT_PLAN.md or chat history to learn the final decisions.

## Acceptance
Every downstream implementation bead can cite an exact decision contained in its own copied context; all eight open decisions from the project plan are resolved or deliberately deferred with a gate; no broad coding is authorized while this bead remains open.
acceptance_criteria: ADR разрешает или явно откладывает все восемь open decisions, согласован с upstream invariants, содержит rejected alternatives и разрешает downstream implementation только после нескольких review passes.

title: Define upstream contribution, naming, and synchronization strategy
description:
## Background
The repository is a real GitHub fork and must preserve MIT attribution and an understandable relationship to treygoff24/papercuts. Generic fixes should be separable from ACFS-specific adapters. The crates.io name and binary compatibility cannot be assumed.

## Work
Define remote-sync procedure, upstream tag/fork compatibility matrix, commit separation rules, criteria for opening upstream PRs, policy for carrying rejected changes, fork naming/version/package strategy, and release notes language. Cover how to fetch/rebase or merge without rewriting public history and how to recover from upstream divergence.

## Acceptance
A maintainer runbook provides preflight, sync commands, conflict handling, verification, rollback, and evidence. The plan clearly states when the binary/crate keeps or changes its name and prevents accidental publication to a package namespace we do not control.
acceptance_criteria: Есть runbook sync/PR/divergence, package/name policy, compatibility matrix и rollback; MIT attribution сохранена; запрещена публикация в неподтверждённый namespace.
notes:
Baseline audit: docs/UPSTREAM_V0_1_COMPATIBILITY_AUDIT.md.
Live snapshot: upstream/main ffba2bd453ab0faeadf4f923fc727586958c8d6f; v0.1.0 tag 5d8b827abbd054f5f506d26be865f5b7f573a298; issue #1 open without comments on 2026-07-11. Generic upstream candidates: risk docs, rust-version/MSRV docs+CI, removal/replacement of the dangling absolute skill symlink. Fork decisions: safe default, required path fields, sensitive-input refusal, and strict repo validation. Preserve MIT attribution and do not assume crates.io namespace ownership.

title: Implement safe profile and storage resolution
description:
## Outcome
Implement the approved safe/private storage default and explicit legacy compatibility path while preserving deterministic discovery and structured errors.

## Scope
Add the smallest required CLI/env/config surface; keep resolution centralized and visible through schema/meta; ensure read-only commands never create a file or directory; keep mutation annotations honest. Handle git/non-git roots, worktrees, missing HOME, explicit missing paths, permissions, empty env values, and existing logs. Do not add a server or ambient network dependency.

## Tests and evidence
Add unit tables for precedence and edge cases, black-box tests for first-run and migration behavior, and real CLI acceptance using isolated temp repos. Tests must assert filesystem side effects, exit codes, envelope destinations, and detailed diagnostic context without leaking private values. Include preflight, execution, verification, rollback/disable command, and evidence path in closeout.

## Acceptance
Safe default and legacy mode behave exactly as the approved ADR; upstream invariants remain green; schema documents every mutation/read-only distinction; cargo gate and scoped UBS pass.
design: Likely ownership: CLI/env resolution and discovery seam (src/cli.rs, src/store.rs or a dedicated profile module). Establish stable interfaces before parallel path/secret work. Reserve overlapping files through Agent Mail. Avoid changing fold/locking unless the ADR requires it.
acceptance_criteria: Safe default и legacy mode соответствуют ADR; read-only commands не создают state; precedence и edge cases покрыты unit/black-box tests; schema честна; cargo gate и UBS зелёные.

title: Implement privacy-preserving path and project metadata
description:
## Outcome
Stop safe-mode records and errors from exposing unnecessary absolute cwd/repository paths while retaining the approved amount of useful project context.

## Scope
Implement the selected omit/relative/project-key contract, serialization and list output behavior, compatibility handling for existing records, worktree/symlink cases, non-repo use, and redacted diagnostics. Preserve append-only history: do not rewrite old journals. Do not substitute reversible hashing for real minimization.

## Tests and evidence
Unit-test normalization and boundary cases; black-box add/list/schema/doctor in safe and legacy modes; include paths with usernames, spaces, Unicode, symlinks, worktrees, and outside-repo cwd. Assertions must prove forbidden absolute fragments are absent from stdout, stderr, and journal bytes. Provide rollback via compatibility mode or focused commit revert.

## Acceptance
Approved record examples match byte-for-byte where determinism applies; old contract-1 data remains readable per ADR; no hidden path is echoed; full Rust gate and scoped UBS pass.
design: Prefer a dedicated metadata/path policy module with pure functions. Integrate through CutRecord construction and output serialization; avoid changing append/fold logic. Coordinate any src/commands/add.rs or src/lib.rs edits with the secret-preflight bead.
acceptance_criteria: Safe mode не оставляет запрещённых absolute fragments в journal/stdout/stderr; старые records читаются; deterministic examples совпадают; path edge cases, rollback, cargo gate и UBS доказаны.

title: Implement local sensitive-data preflight with redacted diagnostics
description:
## Outcome
Prevent common accidental secret/PII-like material from being appended under the approved policy, without claiming exhaustive detection or leaking the detected value in diagnostics.

## Scope
Implement the deterministic bounded scanner, policy modes, override/dry-run behavior, stable error codes, redacted reason categories, and schema documentation defined by the ADR. Keep scanning offline and fast. Avoid logging raw matches. Ensure the append lock/store path is not entered after a refusal and no file/directory is created solely by a rejected attempt where the contract forbids it.

## Tests and evidence
Use synthetic fixtures only. Table-test true positives and false positives across credentials, URLs, PEM, identifiers, benign hashes, multiline/Unicode/large input, and encoded limitations. Add black-box tests proving secrets are absent from stdout, stderr, and filesystem. Include performance bounds and override audit behavior. Run all tests five times only if shared store/concurrency code changes.

## Acceptance
Policy matrix is fully represented; outputs are stable and machine-readable; no suspect value is echoed; limitations are documented; cargo gate, adversarial test corpus, and scoped UBS pass.
design: Prefer a dedicated pure sensitive-data policy module returning category-only findings. Integrate before record append and before any forbidden filesystem side effect. Coordinate shared src/commands/add.rs, src/cli.rs, and schema surfaces with path/profile work.
acceptance_criteria: Synthetic adversarial corpus покрыт; refused inputs не персистятся и не эхоятся; override audit и performance bounds проверены; limitations документированы; cargo gate и UBS зелёные.
